title: Compare Quantities in Same Unit
description: Compare two quantities expressed in the same unit to determine which is greater and by how much.
condition: Given two numerical quantities expressed in the same unit.
response: Determine which quantity is greater and optionally compute their difference.
label: Production / Skill
example: Compare $72$ minutes and $70$ minutes: since $72>70$, $72$ minutes is greater by $2$ minutes.
notes: Often follows percent or unit conversions.

title: Compute Percent from Part and Whole
description: Determine what percent a part is of a whole by forming an equivalent ratio with denominator 100 or multiplying the fraction by 100%.
condition: Given a part P and whole W, asked what percent P is of W.
response: Compute (P/W) × 100% or create an equivalent ratio with denominator 100.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Unit Rate by Division
description: Find a unit rate by dividing one quantity by the corresponding quantity of another unit.
condition: Given two related quantities (e.g., distance and time) and asked for a per-1 rate.
response: Divide the dependent quantity by the independent quantity to find the rate per 1 unit.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $240$ miles in $4$ hours, then speed $=240\div4=60$ miles per hour.
notes: Applies to miles/hour, laps/minute, etc. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Convert Minutes to Seconds
description: Convert a time measurement in minutes to seconds using the relationship 1 minute = 60 seconds.
condition: A time in minutes must be expressed in seconds.
response: Multiply the number of minutes by 60.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Based on conventional time conversion factor.

title: Convert Units Using Conversion Factor
description: Convert a measurement from one unit to another using a given multiplicative conversion factor.
condition: Given a quantity in one unit and a conversion rate between units.
response: Multiply (or divide) by the conversion factor to express the quantity in the target unit.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Direction of multiplication must match units.

title: Find Part from Percent
description: Compute the part when given a percent and the whole quantity.
condition: Given a percent $p\%$ and a whole quantity $W$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Whole from Percent and Part
description: Determine the whole quantity when given that a known part represents a given percent of the whole.
condition: Given that $P$ is $p\%$ of an unknown whole.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $30$ is $60\%$ of a trip, then total $=30\div0.6=50$.

title: Generate an Equivalent Ratio
description: Create an equivalent ratio by multiplying or dividing both quantities by the same number.
condition: A ratio a:b is given and a related quantity is desired.
response: Multiply or divide both terms of the ratio by the same factor to form an equivalent ratio.
label: Principle
example: From $4:17$, divide both by $2$ to get $2:8.5$.
notes: Alternative strategy to unit rate; explicitly shown in Q1 sample table.

title: Identify Reciprocal Unit Rate
description: Recognize that a ratio produces two related unit rates by inverting the quantities.
condition: A ratio a:b is given and all unit rates describing the situation are requested.
response: State both a per 1 b and b per 1 a by dividing in both directions.
label: Principle / Model
example: From $3$ dollars for $12$ yards: $3 \div 12 = 0.25$ dollars per yard and $12 \div 3 = 4$ yards per dollar.
notes: Explains why both C and D are correct in Q1.

title: Interpret Unit Rate in Context
description: Match a computed unit rate to a verbal description with correct units.
condition: A numerical unit rate is computed and multiple verbal descriptions with units are given.
response: Select the description whose units and value match the computed unit rate.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If the unit rate is $0.25$ dollars per yard, select "0.25 dollar per yard."
notes: Requires attention to unit order (dollars per yard vs yards per dollar).

title: Recognize 100% as the Whole
description: Understand that 100% represents the entire quantity in a percent context.
condition: A context involving percentages where the whole amount must be identified.
response: Identify the whole quantity as corresponding to 100%.
label: Concept
example: If there are 50 states total, then 50 states corresponds to 100%.

title: Recognize Equivalent Measurement Ratios
description: Understand that two measurements of the same quantity in different units form an equivalent ratio.
condition: A statement gives a measurement equivalence (e.g., 4 liters is 17 cups).
response: Interpret the pair as a ratio that can generate equivalent ratios.
label: Concept
example: "8 kilometers is approximately 5 miles" is interpreted as the ratio $\frac{8 \text{ km}}{5 \text{ miles}}$.

title: Scale a Quantity by Multiplication
description: Multiply a unit rate or proportional value by a given factor to find an equivalent quantity.
condition: A per‑unit amount or proportional relationship is known and a new quantity is given.
response: Multiply the unit rate by the new quantity.
label: Principle
example: If $1$ mile $= 1.6$ km, then $3$ miles $= 3 \times 1.6 = 4.8$ km.
notes: Implements proportional scaling after unit rate is found.

title: Scale Quantity Using Unit Rate
description: Multiply a given quantity by a known unit rate to find a proportional amount.
condition: Given a unit rate and a target quantity in the corresponding unit.
response: Multiply the target quantity by the unit rate.
label: Principle / Model
example: At $60$ miles per hour, time for $90$ miles is $90\div60=1.5$ hours.
notes: May involve multiplication or division depending on rate orientation.

title: Use Equivalent Ratios to Represent Percents
description: Create an equivalent ratio with denominator 100 to represent or interpret a percent relationship.
condition: Given a ratio A:B where B is not 100, and asked to express it as a percent or connect it to percent form.
response: Scale the ratio to an equivalent ratio with 100 as the second term.
label: Principle / Model
example: The ratio 23:50 is equivalent to 46:100, so 23 is 46%.

title: Use Unit Percent Strategy
description: Find the value of 1% or 10% of a quantity and scale up to determine other percentages.
condition: Given a whole corresponding to 100%, asked to find a smaller percent such as 1%, 2%, 7%, or 10%.
response: Divide the whole by 100 (or 10) to find 1% (or 10%), then multiply to reach the target percent.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 50 states is 100%, then 1% is 0.5 states and 7 states = 7 × 2% = 14%.
notes: In Q1, reasoning uses 1 state = 2% since 50 corresponds to 100%.
